Silently Installing Uniface on Windows

It is possible to install Uniface products without user interaction, which may be useful when deploying Uniface applications to multiple users.

The Uniface installer is provided as an electronic distribution (Edist), in the form of a self-extracting and auto-starting executable.

When using the Edist.exe you can run it from the command line with the installer parameters. Alternatively, you can first extract it and then run the extracted installer (setup.exe) from the command line.

Syntax

Path/ Edist.exe {-extract TargetDir | {-r | -s }/f1{InstallParamsFile}/f2 {LogFile} }

  • extract—extract the contents of the distribution into the specified location. This preserves the extracted contents in the specified location and does not start the installer.
  • -r —record the installation parameters in a response file (specified by the /f1 flag). The installer starts interactively and records the provided information and button clicks. If the /f1 flag is not used, the response file is: Setup.iss in the Windows directory.
  • -s —run the installation silently using the values recorded in the response file (specified by the /f1 flag) and create a log file. If the /f1 flag is omitted, a default response file Setup.iss is expected in the same directory as the extracted setup.exe.
  • /f1ResponseFile —complete path and file name of the response file. The /f1 flag can be used with both -r and -s to indicate an alternative location or name for the response file.
  • /f2LogFile —specify an alternative location or name for the log file. If omitted, the default log file is Setup.log.
Note: There must not be any white space between the /f1 and /f2 flags and their value. Double quotes and fully-qualified paths are also required. For example. /f1 C:/setup.iss does not work, but /f1"C:/setup.iss" does.

Silent Installation from the Command Line

  1. Optionally, extract the Edist.exe to your preferred location using the extract command line option. For example:
    swinuf100403w32.exe extract E:/uniface_setup
  2. Run either the Edist or the extracted setup.exe from the command line using the -r (record) option to create an installation parameters file.

    For example, when using the extracted setup.exe:

    E:/uniface_setup/win/setup.exe -r /f1"D:/setup.iss"

    As you follow the prompts of the setup program, these options create a file called setup.iss with the correct installation instructions.

    If you are using the unextracted Edist, use that path instead. For example:

    C:/swinuf100403w32.exe -r /f1"D:/setup.iss"
  3. Run the Edist or the extracted setup.exe program with the -s (silent) options. For example, to install using the previously-created setup.iss file:
    E:/uniface_setup/win/setup.exe -s /f1"D:/setup.iss"
CAUTION: Although the setup parameters file can be edited, this requires knowledge of the format and is not recommended.
Related Links
  • Uniface Installer for Windows

    The Uniface installer prompts you for the information it needs, and provides on-screen guidance to help you make the appropriate choices.

    Procedure to install Uniface Enterprise Edition on Windows.
